Nebraska Medical Nutrition Therapist License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
Compact Enacted - Not Yet Active

Reciprocity in Nebraska for Medical Nutrition Therapists

Nebraska has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. Until implementation goes live, apply by endorsement through the state board.

Once active, compact participation should reduce or eliminate the need for a separate transfer application.

Endorsement fee
$114
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Not listed
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD or RDN status satisfies this and the education requirements.

  3. Submit the Application to DHHS

    Complete the application form for Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure and submit it with the $114 fee, proof of age (minimum 19), and proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence.

  4. Provide Verification & Fingerprints

    Send a copy of your active CDR card or have primary source verification sent. Starting January 1, 2026, all DHHS applicants must undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$114
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Renewal fee
$114
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (by Sept 1 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Verification: Verification of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Proof of Age & Legal Presence: A copy of a birth certificate, driver's license, passport, or similar documentation.
  • Fingerprint Background check: Required for all new applications starting in 2026.

Board Contact

Board
Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure
Department
Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
Phone
(402) 471-2115
Email
dhhs.licensure2117@nebraska.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $114. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years (by Sept 1 of odd years) + 30 CE hours.

Nebraska Medical Nutrition Therapist Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Nebraska LMNT license cost?

The initial license application fee is $114.

When does the Nebraska license need to be renewed?

Nebraska LMNT licenses expire on September 1 of all odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $114.

Is the title 'Dietitian' licensed in Nebraska?

No, Nebraska licenses the specific practice of Medical Nutrition Therapy, granting the title Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT). General nutrition services may not require a license, but only an LMNT can provide medical nutrition therapy.

Does Nebraska have reciprocity for medical nutrition therapists?

Nebraska has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, medical nutrition therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my medical nutrition therapist license to Nebraska?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
